title: alstroemeria graminea
intro: Alstroemeria graminea, also known as the grass-leaved lily or Mexican gold, is a species of flowering plant in the Alstroemeria genus. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Alstroemeria graminea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and promote wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared by drying the flowers and leaves, which can be made into teas, infusions, or used in salves and ointments for topical application.
